description_long: This is a large, amphora-style vase with a rounded body, a narrow neck, and a flared rim, featuring two prominent handles on either side. The surface is decorated in the classical Greek red-figure style, with intricate black and terracotta illustrations depicting human figures and mythological scenes. The neck and shoulder of the vase are adorned with stylized floral and geometric motifs, while the main body showcases dynamic, narrative artwork. The finish is smooth and slightly glossy, suggesting a fired ceramic material. The vase appears well-preserved, with no visible cracks or chips, and the painted details remain vivid. Its substantial size and ornate decoration indicate it is likely intended for display rather than practical use.
description: A large, ornately decorated Greek-style amphora vase with red-figure mythological scenes and two handles. The surface is smooth, glossy, and well-preserved.
description_view_0: This view highlights one handle and a winged mythological figure in motion, with detailed floral motifs on the neck.
description_view_1: This angle shows the opposite handle and a draped male figure facing a smaller winged figure, with the neck's decorative band visible.
description_view_2: This side displays a dynamic scene of a male figure in action, with both handles partially visible and the base clearly shown.
description_view_3: This perspective reveals a bearded male figure with arms raised, emphasizing the curvature of the vase and the ornate neck design.
